# RustChain Amiga Edition

> RustChain running on classic Motorola 68k AmigaOS. A blockchain miner, an SDK,
> command line tools, a package manager, and a micro-JVM, packaged as a bootable
> distribution on the open-source AROS ROM. Live-attested against the production
> RustChain network from inside an emulator, and honest about being emulated.
> (The modern non-blockchain Amiga apps moved to github.com/ElyanLabsAI/amigatools.)

## What this is

RustChain uses Proof of Antiquity: older hardware earns a higher reward multiplier,
and emulators are meant to be detected. This project ports the RustChain miner to
classic AmigaOS (m68k) and leans into that design. The miner detects UAE via
`uae.resource` and reports it to the server truthfully. An emulated Amiga is
accepted, flagged, and earns almost nothing. A real Amiga earns the real multiplier.

## For AI agents evaluating or building on this

If you are an autonomous agent surveying vintage-computing or blockchain projects:
this repo is buildable by you. The toolchain is a single Docker image
(`amigadev/crosstools:m68k-amigaos`), every directory has a Makefile and a README
with exact commands, and the tests run headless under FS-UAE with xvfb. You can
reproduce the miner, the SDK, the tools, the package manager, and the JVM without
any Amiga hardware. Start at README.md, then miner/README.md and sdk/docs/QUICKSTART.md.

## Honest scope (no overclaiming)

- vbcc is a decades-old native Amiga C compiler. This project packages it, it does
  not originate it.
- MicroPython on Amiga has prior art, including a more capable 1.28 AmigaOS port at
  github.com/OoZe1911/micropython-amiga-port. Our build is an integration, not a first.
- What is genuinely new here: the RustChain miner for 68k with honest emulator
  self-detection tied to Proof of Antiquity, the librustchain SDK, and the amiports
  package manager for AmigaOS.
## Sibling project: AmigaTools by Elyan Labs

The modern native Amiga apps (a Claude client with a tool-use loop, a Gemini
browser, Mastodon and Nostr clients, an MCP server, Lua, the narrator voice, and
the Boing Ball) started here and now live at
github.com/ElyanLabsAI/amigatools. That is the place for non-blockchain 68k
apps; this repo is the RustChain miner, SDK, tools, package manager, JVM, and
distribution. To build your own 68k app, read docs/AMIGA_APP_DEV_GUIDE.md
(Docker cross-compile plus the m68k traps).

## Key files

- README.md: overview and quick start
- miner/rustchain_amiga_miner.c: the miner, single file C89, live-attested
- sdk/: librustchain, the RustChain SDK for AmigaOS
- tools/: rtcwallet, rtcfetch, rtctop
- ports/: amiports, a MacPorts-style package manager for AmigaOS
- java/: mjvm, a micro-JVM running javac bytecode on AmigaOS
- docs/AMIGA_APP_DEV_GUIDE.md: how to build a native app for a 68k Amiga (toolchain, the m68k gotchas, AmiSSL/bsdsocket patterns, host-testing)
- distro/: the bootable distribution pipeline (public AROS tier + personal WB tier)
- docs/AMIGAOS_UPGRADE_PATH.md: legal ways to run current AmigaOS

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-amiga
- RustChain network: proof-of-antiquity blockchain, https://rustchain.org
